title: Introduction to Sustainable Finance Course 2026 | Earn a UN Certificate in Green Finance and Climate Action
summary: As sustainability and climate action continue to shape the future of global economies, professionals, students, policymakers, and businesses are increasingly seeking practical knowledge in sustainable finance. The Introduction to Sustainable Finance course offers learners a comprehensive understanding of how finance can drive sustainable development and support climate goals worldwide. This self-paced online course is designed to provide participants with technical knowledge on sustainable finance, including its role in supporting the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and the Paris Agreement on Climate Change. The programme also explores how sustainable finance principles can be applied in real-world business, government, and investment settings. The course is offered in English and includes a digital certification option for participants who successfully complete all assessments. What is the Introduction to Sustainable Finance Course? The Introduction to Sustainable Finance course is an interactive and practice-oriented online programme originally developed in 2018 and comprehensively updated in 2021. It introduces learners to the foundations of sustainable finance while offering opportunities to explore advanced concepts, tools, frameworks, and global initiatives shaping the future of finance and sustainability. The course was developed through collaboration between major international institutions including: Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GmbH Skandinaviska Enskilda Banken (SEB) Partnership for Action on Green Economy (PAGE) United Nations Environment Programme Finance Initiative (UNEP FI) The programme aims to strengthen climate literacy and equip participants with the knowledge needed to make informed financial and sustainability decisions. Why Sustainable Finance Matters Sustainable finance has become one of the fastest-growing sectors globally as governments, financial institutions, investors, and businesses seek ways to align financial systems with environmental and social goals. This course explains how sustainable finance can contribute to: Climate change mitigation and adaptation Sustainable economic growth Green investments Environmental protection Poverty reduction Inclusive development Clean energy transitions Resilient infrastructure development Participants will also understand how sustainability considerations influence financial decision-making across industries and institutions. Course Duration and Format Course Type: Self-paced online course Duration: Approximately 10 hours Language: English Access Mode: Online Certification: Available upon successful completion Certificate Fee: USD 20 Because the programme is self-paced, participants can learn according to their own schedules and complete modules at their convenience. Learning Objectives By the end of the course, participants will be able to: Explain the contribution of sustainable finance toward achieving: Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Paris Agreement climate goals Understand key sustainable finance concepts and frameworks. Describe how sustainability considerations impact financial sector decisions. Identify the role of regulations and industry initiatives in shaping sustainable finance. Understand different sustainable finance instruments such as: Green bonds Sustainable loans Sustainability-linked products Apply sustainable finance principles in policy and business contexts. UN Digital Certificate Participants who score at least 70% on all quizzes become eligible for a UN digital certificate of completion. Important certificate details: Participants have up to 3 attempts per quiz. Certificate becomes available after successful completion and payment of USD 20. The digital certificate can be downloaded and shared professionally. This certificate provides added value for professionals working in: Banking Development finance ESG consulting Sustainability management Government policy Climate programmes Investment management Who Should Take This Course? The course is designed for participants from diverse sectors including: Government institutions Financial institutions Universities and academia Civil society organizations Businesses and corporations Climate professionals Sustainability practitioners Development organizations Students and researchers It is especially valuable for individuals interested in: ESG investing Climate finance Sustainable development Green economy transitions Corporate sustainability Key Benefits of the Course Participants will benefit from: 1. Global Recognition Receive a UN-affiliated certificate that strengthens professional credibility in sustainability and finance. 2. Flexible Learning Complete the course online at your own pace from anywhere in the world. 3. Industry-Relevant Knowledge Gain practical insights into sustainable finance instruments and frameworks used globally. 4. Career Advancement Enhance opportunities in sustainability, banking, ESG, climate policy, and development sectors. 5. Climate Literacy Develop deeper understanding of how financial systems influence environmental and social outcomes. Institutions Behind the Course The course reflects collaboration among globally recognized organizations advancing sustainable finance and green economy initiatives. Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GIZ works globally with governments and institutions to promote sustainable development and innovative financial systems. Skandinaviska Enskilda Banken (SEB) SEB is a pioneer in green bond development and sustainable finance innovation. UNEP Finance Initiative (UNEP FI) UNEP FI collaborates with financial institutions worldwide to accelerate sustainable finance. Partnership for Action on Green Economy (PAGE) PAGE supports countries in integrating sustainability into economic policy and development planning. How to Enroll Interested participants can enroll directly through the official course platform. Apply Here Why You Should Apply As global economies increasingly prioritize sustainability and climate resilience, sustainable finance knowledge is becoming essential across industries. This course provides a strong foundation for anyone seeking to understand: Green investments Climate finance ESG integration Sustainable banking Environmental risk management Sustainable development financing Whether you are a student, policymaker, entrepreneur, banker, or sustainability professional, this course offers practical knowledge that aligns with current global trends and future opportunities. For more information on this course visit here Discover more short courses opportunities on OFY on here The post Introduction to Sustainable Finance Course 2026 | Earn a UN Certificate in Green Finance and Climate Action appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Ingazi Youth Ambassadors Cohort 2: Leadership Opportunity for Rwandan Youth (Ages 18–30)
summary: Applications are now open for Ingazi Youth Ambassadors – Cohort 2 , a youth leadership initiative designed to empower young people in Rwanda to become changemakers in their communities. This program seeks motivated youth leaders who are ready to take action, inspire others, and contribute to positive social and community development across Rwanda. About the Ingazi Youth Ambassadors Program The Ingazi Youth Ambassadors program identifies and supports young leaders who are passionate about driving meaningful change at the local level. Selected ambassadors will be part of a cohort that promotes leadership, civic engagement, and community-driven impact initiatives. Why This Program Matters Youth leadership is a key driver of sustainable community development. This initiative equips young people with the platform to: Engage directly in community transformation Develop leadership and communication skills Inspire peers and contribute to local solutions Participate in youth-led impact initiatives Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to: Young people from Rwanda Ages 18 to 30 years Individuals passionate about leadership and community development Youth ready to take active roles in creating local impact Application Deadline All applications must be submitted by: 15 May 2026 How to Apply Apply here. For more Training Opportuinities , Visit OFY here. The post Ingazi Youth Ambassadors Cohort 2: Leadership Opportunity for Rwandan Youth (Ages 18–30) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Youth Advisory Committee 2026: Opportunity for Young Leaders in Ethiopia, Ghana, Nigeria, and Rwanda
summary: Applications are now open for the Youth Advisory Committee , a pan-African initiative designed to elevate youth voices in shaping entrepreneurship and economic empowerment programs across the continent. Delivered through a partnership with the Mastercard Foundation , this initiative empowers young people to actively influence program design, ensuring that interventions better reflect the realities, challenges, and aspirations of youth in Africa. About the Youth Advisory Committee The Youth Advisory Committee brings together young leaders from Ethiopia, Ghana, Nigeria, and Rwanda to co-create solutions that strengthen youth entrepreneurship and economic inclusion programs. Members will play a direct role in shaping initiatives that support young people to thrive, innovate, and access meaningful opportunities. What Committee Members Will Do Selected youth advisors will: Share lived experiences and insights on youth challenges Identify barriers to entrepreneurship and employment Contribute to program design and decision-making processes Co-create inclusive and impactful solutions Support the development of youth-centered economic empowerment initiatives Why This Opportunity Matters Youth participation is essential in designing effective development programs. This initiative ensures that young people are not only beneficiaries but also active contributors to shaping solutions that affect their future. By joining the committee, participants will help build more inclusive systems that improve access to entrepreneurship and economic opportunities across Africa. Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to young people from: Ethiopia Ghana Nigeria Rwanda Applicants should: Be young people with lived experience relevant to entrepreneurship or economic empowerment Be passionate about youth development and inclusion Be willing to contribute ideas and collaborate with others Have interest in shaping programs that support young people’s economic participation Application Deadline Applications must be submitted by: May 12, 2026 How to Apply Apply here For more Policy opportunities, Visit OFY here. The post Youth Advisory Committee 2026: Opportunity for Young Leaders in Ethiopia, Ghana, Nigeria, and Rwanda appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Re-D Fund 2026: Microgrants for Civil Society Organizations Advancing Digital Democracy in East and Southeast Asia
summary: The Tifa Foundation has launched the Re-D Fund, a major regional funding initiative designed to strengthen digital democracy, civic participation, human rights advocacy, and organizational resilience across East and Southeast Asia. The Re-D Fund, which stands for “Reimagining Futures for Digital Democracy,” provides financial and non-financial support to civil society groups using digital technologies to address shrinking civic space and growing barriers to democratic participation in the digital era. The initiative seeks to empower marginalized and underrepresented groups by providing microgrants and ecosystem support for projects that strengthen civic freedoms, inclusive governance, advocacy, and democratic innovation. The programme is expected to support at least 100 local collaborators across the region, with a strong emphasis on women, youth, grassroots organizations, Indigenous Peoples, and gender minorities. About the Re-D Fund The Re-D Fund is a strategic grant mechanism developed to: Expand civic participation through digital tools Advance rights-based reforms Strengthen organizational resilience Support marginalized communities Promote democratic innovation Improve access to funding for underrepresented civil society actors The fund responds directly to challenges such as: Shrinking civic space Digital repression Funding inequities Exclusion of grassroots organizations from traditional grant systems Increasing restrictions on democratic participation By providing accessible financial support and ecosystem-building opportunities, the Re-D Fund aims to strengthen civil society’s role in protecting democracy and human rights in the digital age. Main Objectives of the Re-D Fund The programme seeks to ensure that local civil society groups in East and Southeast Asia gain equitable access to: Financial resources Capacity-building opportunities Support ecosystems Advocacy platforms Digital democracy tools The ultimate goal is to enable organizations to develop and implement strategies that strengthen democratic participation and civic resilience amid digital-era challenges. Who the Re-D Fund Supports The Re-D Fund prioritizes support for marginalized and underrepresented groups, including: Women-led organizations Youth groups Informal and grassroots movements Indigenous Peoples Gender minorities Community-based advocacy groups Civil society networks At least 50% of supported subgrantees are expected to come from marginalized communities. Eligible Organizations and Groups Eligible applicants include: Civil society groups Grassroots collectives Community organizations Informal advocacy groups Civic networks Rights-based coalitions Applicants must: Operate in East or Southeast Asia Work in countries categorized as closed, repressed, or obstructed by the CIVICUS Monitor Be eligible for OECD DAC Official Development Assistance Have operated for at least 3 years Be legally registered or partnered with a fiscal sponsor Be capable of receiving grant funds directly or via a host organization Priority Countries The Re-D Fund specifically prioritizes projects implemented in: Cambodia Indonesia Laos Malaysia Myanmar Philippines Taiwan Thailand Timor-Leste Vietnam Thematic Focus Areas Projects funded by the Re-D Fund must address civic space and democracy in the digital era through one or more thematic tracks. 1. Advancing Reforms for Civic Freedoms This thematic area supports: Legal reforms Civic freedoms advocacy Democratic governance innovation Policy development Human rights protections Eligible activities include: Legal research Policy research Advocacy campaigns Human rights monitoring Transparency initiatives Policy dialogue forums Democratic innovation activities 2. Enhancing Civic Participation of Marginalized Groups This focus area supports initiatives that: Increase participation of excluded communities Strengthen digital inclusion Promote equitable civic engagement Amplify marginalized voices 3. Strengthening Civic Influence Projects under this category may include: Public engagement strategies Advocacy campaigns Community mobilization Civic technology solutions Coalition-building efforts 4. Fostering Holistic Resilience The programme supports organizational resilience through: Digital security Operational sustainability Community resilience models Crisis preparedness Organizational strengthening 5. Expanding Civil Society Resources This thematic track focuses on: Capacity building Resource-sharing systems Infrastructure development Digital ecosystem support Long-term sustainability Grant Structure The Re-D Fund will support: Two cohorts of subgrantees Approximately 50 subgrantees per cohort Cohort I Project implementation period: March 1 – May 31, 2026 Status: Open Cohort II Project implementation period: July 22 – October 23, 2026 Status: Open Application Deadline Applications for Cohort II must be submitted before: June 5, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early and carefully review all eligibility and thematic requirements. Read about the Download Guidelines and APPLY . Grant Amounts The Re-D Fund provides two funding categories. Single Applications Applicants may receive up to: USD 5,000 Joint Applications (2+ Organizations) Collaborative applications may receive up to: USD 10,000 The joint application category encourages partnerships, coalition-building, and collaborative democratic innovation efforts. Why the Re-D Fund Matters Civil society organizations across East and Southeast Asia increasingly face: Political restrictions Funding barriers Digital surveillance Shrinking civic space Exclusion from decision-making processes The Re-D Fund addresses these challenges by supporting grassroots organizations that often struggle to access traditional funding systems. The initiative recognizes that marginalized communities are frequently the most affected by democratic decline and digital inequality, yet remain among the least funded. Key Benefits of the Re-D Fund Selected organizations benefit from: Direct financial support Increased visibility Ecosystem networking opportunities Support for digital democracy initiatives Access to collaborative regional partnerships Flexible support for innovative civic solutions What Makes This Fund Unique? The Re-D Fund stands out because it: Prioritizes marginalized communities Supports grassroots and informal groups Focuses on digital democracy Encourages collaborative applications Combines financial and non-financial support Emphasizes feminist, inclusive, and rights-based approaches About Tifa Foundation The Tifa Foundation is an Indonesian organization that promotes: Openness Diversity Equality Justice Democratic participation The organization works through: Strategic multi-stakeholder collaboration Local and international partnerships Constructive engagement approaches Resource provision and direct implementation Tifa Foundation plays a critical role in advancing civic participation and democratic resilience across the region. Click HERE to learn more about The Tifa Foundation s Re-D Fund 2026. Official Resources Applicants are encouraged to: Download the official guidelines Review the FAQ section Attend the webinar Carefully prepare application materials Discover more global Grant Opportunities on OFY HERE . The post Re-D Fund 2026: Microgrants for Civil Society Organizations Advancing Digital Democracy in East and Southeast Asia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Eco Champions Germany 2026: Fully Funded Climate Leadership Program for Young Environmental Changemakers Living in Germany
summary: The SOS Children s Villages has officially launched applications for the 2026 Eco Champions Germany Program, an intensive 1.5-year climate leadership and environmental justice initiative designed to empower young people who are passionate about climate action, sustainability, advocacy, and community-driven environmental solutions. The programme seeks five motivated young leaders aged 16–26 who are living in Germany and committed to addressing climate change through activism, innovation, awareness-building, or policy engagement. Through personalized mentoring, financial support, networking opportunities, and international exposure, Eco Champions Germany aims to equip youth with the resources and confidence needed to implement impactful climate-related initiatives within their communities and beyond. Participants will also gain the opportunity to attend a Global Summit in Nigeria and connect with young climate activists from around the world. About the Eco Champions Germany Program Eco Champions Germany is a youth-centered climate and sustainability initiative organized by SOS Children’s Villages. The programme supports young people in: Launching climate-related initiatives Expanding existing environmental projects Advocating for climate justice Building sustainable community solutions Strengthening youth leadership in environmental policy and activism The programme runs for approximately 18 months and combines mentorship, project acceleration, training, financial support, and global collaboration. Main Goals of the Program The Eco Champions initiative seeks to: Empower youth-led environmental action Promote climate justice and sustainability Support marginalized and underrepresented youth Strengthen climate advocacy and leadership Build innovative local solutions to environmental challenges Encourage youth participation in policy and decision-making What Participants Will Receive Selected Eco Champions will benefit from a comprehensive support system designed to help them transform ideas into impactful environmental action. 1. Monthly Seed Funding Participants receive: €300 monthly seed funding This funding supports the implementation and development of participants’ climate and sustainability projects throughout the programme period. 2. Individual Mentoring and Coaching Participants gain access to: Personalized mentoring Leadership guidance Project development support Advocacy coaching 3. Professional Training The programme includes: Workshops Skills development sessions Advocacy training Project management learning opportunities 4. International Networking Opportunities Eco Champions will: Connect with young climate activists globally Engage with international sustainability networks Participate in exchange programmes and conferences 5. Global Summit in Nigeria One of the major highlights of the programme is participation in the: Global Summit in Nigeria Scheduled for September 21–27, 2026 At the summit, participants will meet Eco Champions from other countries and collaborate on global climate discussions. 6. Collaboration Opportunities with Companies Participants may collaborate with companies within the SOS Children’s Villages network to support their initiatives and increase project visibility. 7. Opportunities to Influence Decision-Makers The programme provides: Platforms for youth advocacy Opportunities to engage policymakers Access to national and international climate discussions Who Can Apply? The programme is open to: Young people aged 16–26 Individuals residing in Germany Youth interested in environmental justice and sustainability Young people with project ideas or ongoing initiatives Climate activists and policy advocates Applicants should be able to: Communicate in English Participate actively for approximately 5–10 hours monthly Attend workshops and programme activities Participate in the Nigeria Global Summit Priority Groups Encouraged to Apply The programme strongly encourages applications from: Young women and girls Queer youth Refugees and migrants Young people experiencing racism Youth from low-income households Individuals with disabilities Young people without stable family support Youth from non-academic households Young people from structurally disadvantaged regions Other marginalized or underrepresented communities Do you need help with your application? Then register here for the online workshop! Focus Areas for Climate-Related Projects The programme accepts a broad range of environmental initiatives including: Climate Protection Projects Examples include: CO₂ reduction initiatives Sustainability innovation projects Renewable energy awareness Environmental Education Projects focused on: Climate literacy Awareness campaigns Youth environmental education Advocacy and Policy Engagement Including: Climate activism Political advocacy Policy awareness campaigns Community Engagement Projects that: Mobilize communities Encourage youth participation Build sustainable local solutions Programme Timeline Application Opens April 20, 2026 Application Deadline May 20, 2026 Online Interviews Until June 17, 2026 Participant Notification By June 24, 2026 Programme Start July 1, 2026 Global Summit in Nigeria September 21–27, 2026 Programme Duration 18 months (1.5 years) Program Structure Phase 1: Introduction and Preparation July – September 2026 Orientation sessions Community-building meetings Global Summit preparation Phase 2: Global Summit September 2026 International collaboration Networking with global Eco Champions Climate leadership engagement Phase 3: Mentoring and Accelerator Phase From October 2026 onward Project implementation support Continuous mentoring Funding support Advocacy development The Program’s Inclusive and Anti-Discrimination Approach Eco Champions Germany emphasizes: Feminist principles Anti-racist values Inclusion and accessibility Respectful collaboration Safe participation spaces The programme actively works to reduce participation barriers and accommodate diverse realities and support needs. Click HERE to start your application! Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) FAQ 1: Do participants need to pay to apply or participate? No. The application and participation are completely free. All programme-related costs, including training, mentoring, exchange activities, and the Global Summit, are fully covered. FAQ 2: How much funding do participants receive? Participants receive: €300 per month in seed funding to support their climate and sustainability projects throughout the programme duration. FAQ 3: Is the programme flexible for students or working youth? Yes. The programme aims to remain flexible and supportive of participants balancing: Education Employment Care responsibilities Other commitments FAQ 4: What kinds of projects qualify? Eligible initiatives may include: Climate awareness campaigns Advocacy work Sustainability innovations Educational projects Environmental activism Community engagement programmes FAQ 5: Are online interviews required? Yes. Shortlisted applicants will participate in online interviews. The organization also offers support for applicants experiencing internet or device challenges. FAQ 6: Who evaluates the applications? Applications are reviewed by a team of SOS Children’s Villages staff members from around the world. FAQ 7: What selection criteria are used? Applications are assessed based on: Personal story Commitment to climate action Community relevance Leadership potential Previous impact Originality and innovation Why This Program Matters Eco Champions Germany goes beyond traditional youth programmes by providing: Long-term support Funding access International exposure Leadership development Advocacy opportunities Climate justice engagement The initiative recognizes that young people are essential drivers of environmental change and seeks to amplify their voices and solutions. Learn more about the Climate Leadership Program for Young Environmental Changemakers Living in Germany HERE . Official Contact Information For questions about the programme, applicants may contact: ecochampions@sos-kd.org Discover more global Fellowship and Climate Action opportunities on OFY. The post Eco Champions Germany 2026: Fully Funded Climate Leadership Program for Young Environmental Changemakers Living in Germany appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Visa Africa Accelerator Program Cohort 6 for African FinTech Startups 2026 | Apply Now
summary: Applications are now officially open for the Visa Accelerator Program Cohort 6, a transformative initiative designed to support innovative FinTech startups across Africa. The Visa Accelerator Program is focused on empowering startups that are shaping the future of financial technology by providing mentorship, technical support, industry expertise, training opportunities, and access to a strong innovation ecosystem. This accelerator is ideal for African startups looking to scale innovative financial solutions, improve payment systems, strengthen digital finance infrastructure, and drive financial inclusion across the continent. Whether you are building solutions in digital payments, embedded finance, SME finance, open banking, mobile money, cross-border remittances, or sustainable finance, the Visa Accelerator Program offers a major platform to grow your startup and accelerate your impact. About the Visa Accelerator Program The Visa Accelerator Program is a startup growth initiative aimed at helping African FinTech innovators transform their ideas into scalable businesses. The program is designed to: Strengthen Africa’s FinTech ecosystem Promote digital financial innovation Support entrepreneurship and startup growth Encourage financial inclusion Enhance digital payment infrastructure Help startups expand into African markets The accelerator provides startups with practical business knowledge, expert mentorship, Visa FinTech training, networking opportunities, and industry exposure. What Startups Will Gain From the Visa Accelerator Program Selected startups will benefit from a comprehensive support structure designed to help founders scale smarter and faster. 1. Expert Knowledge and Industry Insights Participants will gain access to: Product development strategies Financial management training Sales and growth strategies Marketing expertise Business scaling insights Customer acquisition approaches The program equips startups with practical knowledge needed to compete effectively in Africa’s rapidly evolving FinTech ecosystem. 2. Personalized Mentorship Startups will receive: One-on-one mentorship sessions Guidance from successful founders Advice from industry experts Strategic business support Operational insights This mentorship component is designed to help startups avoid common mistakes and accelerate sustainable growth. 3. Visa FinTech Training Participants will also gain access to: Visa Developer Portal Technical resources FinTech solution expertise Industry tools and integrations Digital payment insights This training helps startups better understand payment systems and build scalable FinTech products. Who Should Apply? The accelerator is open to startups that meet the following criteria: Startup Stage Eligible startups include: Seed-stage startups Early-stage companies Series A startups Applicants must already have: A Minimum Viable Product (MVP) or A market-ready solution Product and Innovation Focus The program is seeking startups developing innovative solutions in areas such as: Unlocking New Payment Flows Cross-border payments Digital transactions Government payment systems Mobile money interoperability Embedded Finance Financial services integration Digital banking experiences API-enabled financial solutions Empowering Merchants and SMEs SME financing Merchant payment tools Business finance management Payment Infrastructure Payment gateways Digital finance infrastructure Financial technology enablers Future of Finance Open banking Account-to-account payments Social commerce Creator economy finance Sustainable and Inclusive Finance Financial inclusion tools Green finance solutions Inclusive digital banking products Traction Requirement Applicants should have: Market-tested products Early customer traction Evidence of startup growth Existing user engagement Geographic Eligibility The program strongly encourages applications from startups: Operating within Africa Expanding into African markets Committed to Africa’s financial ecosystem Why This Program Matters for African Startups Africa’s FinTech ecosystem continues to grow rapidly, creating major opportunities for startups solving challenges in: Digital payments Financial inclusion SME financing Mobile banking Cross-border transactions Open banking Digital commerce The Visa Accelerator Program provides startups with the strategic support needed to move from early-stage innovation to scalable impact. By joining the accelerator, startups gain access to: Industry credibility Global expertise Investor visibility Business partnerships Technical resources Pan-African networking opportunities Countries Represented in Current and Previous Cohorts The accelerator has already supported startups from several African countries, including: Ghana Nigeria Kenya South Africa Uganda Tanzania Morocco Côte d Ivoire Algeria Egypt Ethiopia Rwanda Zambia Zimbabwe Botswana Cameroon Namibia Tunisia Mauritius Democratic Republic of Congo This demonstrates the program’s strong commitment to supporting innovation across the African continent. Strategic Partners Supporting the Program The accelerator collaborates with notable partners including: AWS Bank of Africa EY FirstBank Onafriq Plug and Play These partnerships strengthen the support ecosystem available to participating startups. How to Apply Interested startups can apply through the official Visa Accelerator Program application portal. Apply Here Application Deadline The official application deadline was not stated on the provided page. Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible to avoid missing updates or selection timelines. Final Thoughts The Visa Accelerator Program Cohort 6 represents a major opportunity for African FinTech startups seeking growth, mentorship, technical support, and regional expansion. For startups building innovative financial solutions in Africa, this accelerator could become a critical stepping stone toward scaling impact, attracting partnerships, and strengthening long-term business sustainability. For more information on this visit here Discover more accelerator program opportunities on OFY here The post Visa Africa Accelerator Program Cohort 6 for African FinTech Startups 2026 | Apply Now appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Youth for COP 2026 Program Opens Applications: Join a Global Climate Learning Journey for Young Changemakers
summary: The third edition of the Youth for COP Program is officially live, offering young people around the world an exciting opportunity to explore global climate conversations, climate diplomacy, and international negotiations connected to the Conference of Parties (COP). Designed as a dynamic climate education and engagement platform, Youth for COP 2026 empowers young people with the knowledge, skills, networks, and confidence needed to meaningfully engage in climate action and policy discussions at local, regional, and global levels. The program combines expert-led learning sessions, interactive workshops, online modules, simulations, and networking opportunities to help participants understand how global climate systems work and how youth voices can contribute to shaping climate solutions. Most importantly, the program is completely free of cost and provides selected participants with certificates and opportunities to explore and engage with COP31 and the wider global climate ecosystem. What is Youth for COP 2026? Youth for COP is an international climate engagement initiative created to bridge the gap between young people and global climate policymaking. Since launching in 2023, the program has evolved into a vibrant youth platform bringing together climate advocates, students, young professionals, researchers, and emerging leaders from across the world. Now entering its third edition, Youth for COP continues to expand its impact by helping participants: Understand international climate negotiations Learn how COP processes work Build climate communication skills Develop digital advocacy capacity Connect with a global network of young climate leaders The initiative is especially valuable for young people interested in climate policy, sustainability, environmental justice, advocacy, diplomacy, youth participation, and global governance. Why Youth for COP Matters Climate change is one of the defining challenges of our generation, yet many young people still struggle to access spaces where climate policies and negotiations are discussed. Youth for COP addresses this gap by creating accessible learning opportunities that: Simplify complex climate negotiations Build climate literacy Encourage youth participation Strengthen youth voices in climate spaces Foster collaboration across countries and cultures The program recognizes that meaningful climate action requires informed and empowered young leaders who can engage critically and constructively in global conversations. What Participants Will Learn The program offers a broad and immersive learning experience focused on international climate governance and practical advocacy skills. Key Learning Areas Include: 1. Understanding COP and Climate Negotiations Participants will learn: What COP is How UN climate negotiations work How climate agreements are shaped The role of governments, civil society, and youth in climate discussions 2. Climate Policy and Global Governance The program explores: International climate frameworks Climate justice Adaptation and mitigation Loss and damage discussions Global climate priorities 3. Communication and Digital Advocacy Participants will develop: Public communication skills Climate storytelling techniques Digital advocacy strategies Social media engagement skills 4. Youth Leadership and Networking The initiative also focuses on: Leadership development Collaborative learning Cross-cultural engagement Networking with climate-focused youth globally Program Structure and Flow The Youth for COP 2026 program follows a structured learning journey designed to progressively build participants’ understanding and engagement. Step 1: Registration and Application Interested participants must: Register for the program Complete the application form Because seats are limited, participants are selected through a competitive process. Step 2: Knowledge Building Selected participants gain access to: Expert-led sessions Online learning modules Climate policy discussions Educational resources This stage provides foundational understanding of climate negotiations and COP processes. Step 3: Interactive Workshops Participants engage in: Simulations Interactive activities Group discussions Collaborative exercises These workshops help participants apply their knowledge in practical ways. Step 4: Activities and Practical Engagement Participants complete: Short assignments Advocacy activities Communication tasks Learning reflections These exercises strengthen real-world engagement and application. Step 5: Certification Participants who successfully complete the program receive: Certificate of participation Certificate of appreciation The certification recognizes participants’ engagement and commitment to climate learning and advocacy. Benefits of Joining Youth for COP 2026 Participants in the program gain access to multiple educational and networking opportunities. 1. Learn Directly From Experts The program features: Climate experts Policy professionals Researchers Youth leaders Climate advocates Participants gain valuable insights into real-world climate negotiations and global policy discussions. 2. Access Global Opportunities Participants can explore: International climate opportunities Youth climate networks Advocacy platforms Engagement opportunities linked to COP31 3. Build Valuable Skills The program strengthens: Communication skills Leadership abilities Digital advocacy Critical thinking Climate literacy 4. Join a Global Community Participants become part of an international network of young people passionate about climate action and sustainability. Program Reach and Impact Since its launch, Youth for COP has built an impressive global community. Program Statistics Over 3,600 registered participants Participants from 48+ countries More than 40 speakers Collaboration with 25 youth organizations Over 80 climate blogs published More than 350 social media posts created These numbers reflect the growing influence and global reach of the initiative. Participant Voices and Climate Storytelling One of the program’s unique features is its emphasis on youth storytelling and lived experiences. Participants publish blogs exploring: Climate vulnerability Indigenous knowledge Climate justice Sustainability Fashion and climate Community resilience Adaptation and survival These stories amplify youth voices from regions heavily impacted by climate change and contribute to global climate dialogue. Who Can Apply? The program welcomes applications from young people interested in: Climate action Sustainability Climate governance Environmental advocacy Policy engagement Youth leadership Importantly: No prior climate experience is required Students and working professionals can apply Participants from different academic and professional backgrounds are encouraged Program Format The Youth for COP program is designed to be flexible and accessible. Program Features Online learning format Interactive workshops Virtual engagement Flexible participation for students and professionals This structure allows participants from different countries and time zones to engage effectively. Cost of Participation One of the most attractive aspects of the program is that it is: Completely free of cost This ensures accessibility for participants from diverse economic backgrounds. Will Participants Attend COP? While participation in the program does not automatically guarantee attendance at COP, the initiative helps participants: Understand global climate spaces Build climate engagement skills Access opportunities connected to COP31 and related climate platforms How to Apply Interested applicants should: Register through the official platform Complete the application form Submit all required information before seats are filled Because participation is limited, early applications are highly encouraged. Register HERE Why You Should Apply Youth for COP 2026 is more than an educational program—it is a platform for young people to become informed climate advocates and active participants in shaping global climate conversations. Whether you are new to climate advocacy or already active in sustainability work, the program offers a valuable opportunity to: Expand your knowledge Strengthen your voice Build international networks Develop practical advocacy skills Engage with global climate conversations in meaningful ways For young changemakers passionate about climate action, Youth for COP 2026 could be a transformative experience. For more information about this opportunity, visit the official website Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT The post Youth for COP 2026 Program Opens Applications: Join a Global Climate Learning Journey for Young Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026: Australia Opens Up to $100,000 Funding for Hands-On STEM Learning Projects
summary: Organizations across Australia now have an exciting opportunity to secure significant funding through the Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026, a national initiative designed to strengthen creativity, innovation, and hands-on STEM learning among young people. The program provides grants ranging from $20,000 to $100,000 to support community-based STEM projects that help students and youth under 18 years old develop practical skills in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics through inquiry-based and experiential learning activities. The initiative particularly focuses on increasing participation among groups that have historically been underrepresented in STEM education and careers, ensuring more young people across Australia gain access to meaningful STEM opportunities. About the Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026 The Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants are part of Australia’s broader effort to promote innovation, scientific literacy, and technological capability among young people. The grants support organizations that deliver engaging STEM activities outside traditional classroom settings. These projects encourage experimentation, creativity, problem-solving, design thinking, engineering skills, and entrepreneurship through interactive and hands-on experiences. Rather than relying solely on theoretical instruction, the program emphasizes practical learning approaches where students actively build, create, test, and explore STEM concepts. Funding Amount Available Successful applicants can receive: Grants from $20,000 to $100,000 The funding is intended to help organizations design and deliver impactful STEM engagement initiatives for young people under 18 years of age. Application Deadline Applications for the Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026 are currently open. Closing Date Wednesday, 17 June 2026 Time: 5:00 PM AEST (Australian Eastern Standard Time) Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications early and ensure all required supporting documentation is complete before submission. Purpose of the Program The Maker Projects Grants aim to: Foster creativity and inquiry-based learning Improve STEM knowledge and practical skills Increase access to STEM opportunities Encourage innovation and entrepreneurial thinking Inspire young people to pursue future STEM studies and careers The program recognizes the growing importance of STEM education in preparing future generations for evolving industries, digital transformation, and technological advancement. Key Objectives of the Grant Opportunity The program has several important objectives designed to improve STEM participation and educational outcomes among young Australians. 1. Deliver Hands-On STEM Learning Projects should allow students and youth under 18 years old to: Experiment with technologies Explore engineering and programming concepts Participate in maker activities Develop practical STEM skills The focus is on active participation and experiential learning. 2. Encourage Creativity and Innovation The program supports initiatives that: Promote creative problem-solving Encourage entrepreneurial thinking Build confidence in STEM-related activities Inspire innovation through practical engagement 3. Increase Interest in STEM Careers Projects should help participants: Discover future career pathways Build interest in STEM subjects Understand real-world applications of technology and science This long-term approach helps strengthen Australia’s future STEM workforce. 4. Improve Accessibility and Inclusion A major focus of the grants is ensuring STEM opportunities are accessible to young people who may face barriers to participation. The program particularly supports projects serving: Regional communities Rural areas Remote communities Economically disadvantaged groups Priority Groups Supported by the Program The Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants place special emphasis on increasing participation among groups historically underrepresented in STEM. These include: 1. Women and Girls Projects that encourage female participation in STEM learning and leadership are strongly supported. 2. First Nations Peoples Programs that engage Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander young people are encouraged. 3. Young People in Regional, Rural, and Remote Areas The grants seek to improve STEM access outside major metropolitan areas. 4. Culturally and Linguistically Diverse (CALD) Communities Projects supporting inclusive STEM participation among multicultural communities are welcomed. 5. People with Disability Inclusive STEM learning opportunities for young people with disabilities are prioritized. 6. Young People from Low Socio-Economic or Educationally Disadvantaged Backgrounds The program supports initiatives that reduce barriers and create equal learning opportunities. Expected Outcomes of the Program The Maker Projects Grants aim to generate long-term positive outcomes for young people and communities across Australia. Anticipated Outcomes Include: Increased STEM knowledge and practical skills Greater participation in STEM-related activities Improved innovation and entrepreneurial thinking Increased accessibility to quality STEM learning experiences Stronger engagement with science and technology pathways Long-term development of inquiry-based learning skills Who Can Apply? Organizations may apply if they meet the eligibility criteria outlined in the grant guidelines. Applicants must demonstrate: Eligibility as an approved entity An eligible STEM project Eligible project expenditure The complete eligibility requirements are detailed in the official grant opportunity guidelines. Eligible Projects Projects funded under this program should: Deliver community-based STEM engagement activities Target students and youth under 18 years old Use hands-on and inquiry-based learning approaches Encourage experimentation and practical skill development Examples may include: STEM workshops Coding and robotics programs Maker spaces Engineering challenges Innovation labs Technology camps Science engagement events How to Apply Organizations interested in applying should carefully prepare before starting the application process. Application Process Step 1: Review the Grant Guidelines Applicants should thoroughly read and understand: Eligibility rules Funding conditions Assessment criteria Project requirements Step 2: Create or Log Into the Online Portal Applications must be submitted through the official online grants portal. Step 3: Complete the Application Applicants should provide: Detailed project information Supporting evidence Budget details Impact goals Organizational information Step 4: Submit Before the Deadline Applications must be fully completed before submission. Incomplete or incorrect applications may not be considered, and mistakes cannot be corrected after submission. Apply HERE How Applications Are Assessed Applications will be evaluated based on: Alignment with program objectives Potential impact on young people Accessibility and inclusion Project quality and feasibility Community engagement Value for money Strong applications will clearly demonstrate how the project will create meaningful STEM learning outcomes. Why These Grants Matter STEM education is increasingly important in today’s rapidly evolving world. However, many young people still face barriers to accessing quality STEM learning opportunities. The Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants help bridge this gap by supporting organizations that create accessible, engaging, and practical STEM experiences for diverse communities across Australia. By investing in youth STEM engagement today, the program contributes to: Future workforce development Innovation ecosystems Educational equity Economic growth Community empowerment Final Thoughts The Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026 offer a valuable opportunity for organizations passionate about STEM education to expand their impact and empower the next generation of innovators, creators, and problem-solvers. With funding of up to $100,000 available, eligible organizations can design transformative projects that inspire curiosity, creativity, and long-term engagement in science and technology. Organizations working to make STEM education more inclusive, practical, and accessible are strongly encouraged to apply before the June 17, 2026 deadline. For more information about the opportunity, visit the official website Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT The post Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026: Australia Opens Up to $100,000 Funding for Hands-On STEM Learning Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Black Girls Defense Fund – Resistance and Resilience Grant 2026 (Up to $20,000 Funding Opportunity for Southern US Organizations)
summary: The Black Girls Defense Fund has officially opened applications for its Resistance and Resilience Grant , a powerful funding opportunity aimed at strengthening organizations that defend, protect, and advocate for Black girls, women, and femme-identifying youth in the Southern United States . This grant supports community-led initiatives working at the frontlines of justice, safety, health equity, and systemic change. It is designed to fund organizations that are actively addressing structural issues such as gender-based violence, reproductive health inequities, adultification, dehumanization, and harms caused by carceral systems. Grant funding ranges from $5,000 to $20,000 , with a strong emphasis on supporting grassroots movements and organizations deeply rooted in their communities. About the Black Girls Defense Fund The Black Girls Defense Fund is an initiative of the Southern Black Girls and Women’s Consortium. It exists to strengthen and protect movements advancing the rights, dignity, and safety of Black girls and women across the Southern United States. The fund focuses on: Community protection and safety Reproductive and maternal health justice Ending gender-based violence Restorative justice approaches Challenging systemic dehumanization and adultification The initiative prioritizes organizations that are led by and directly accountable to the communities they serve. About the Grant The Resistance and Resilience Grant is a flexible funding opportunity designed to support both new and existing initiatives. It provides general operating support to help organizations sustain and expand their work in areas including: Advocacy campaigns Community programs Justice reform initiatives Health and safety programs Youth empowerment efforts The goal is to strengthen long-term capacity for organizations working with Black girls, women, and femme-identifying youth. Funding Amount Minimum: $5,000 Maximum: $20,000 This funding can be used for general operating support, allowing organizations flexibility in how resources are allocated. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for this grant, applicants must meet all of the following requirements: Organizational Requirements Must be a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization , OR Have a fiscal sponsor with a nonprofit organization Budget Requirement Organization must have an annual budget under $1 million Geographic Focus Applicants must operate in at least one of the following Southern U.S. states: Alabama Arkansas Florida Georgia Kentucky Louisiana Mississippi North Carolina South Carolina Tennessee Virginia West Virginia Texas Eligible Focus Areas Organizations must be working on initiatives that address at least one of the following: Gender-based violence prevention and response Reproductive and maternal health justice Justice system and carceral system reform Restorative justice practices Safety and wellbeing of Black girls and women Advocacy against adultification and dehumanization Key Responsibilities of Funded Work Funded organizations are expected to demonstrate impact through: Program Implementation Designing or continuing initiatives that support Black girls, women, and femme youth Implementing advocacy or direct service programs Strengthening community safety systems Community Engagement Engaging directly with affected communities Incorporating feedback from stakeholders Ensuring culturally responsive programming Impact Measurement Defining clear outcomes and success indicators Tracking program effectiveness and community impact Reporting on progress and learnings Application Requirements Applicants must complete the JustFund Common Application™ and submit supporting documents. Required Documents W9 form Proof of 501(c)(3) status OR fiscal sponsorship agreement Most recent Form 990 Organizational budget Application Questions Overview Applicants will be required to respond to detailed questions covering: 1. Program Design and Impact Description of the initiative Activities and implementation plan Expected outcomes and metrics Organizational background and leadership history 2. Community Engagement Strategy How community input is collected and integrated How programs are sustained or expanded Media engagement preferences 3. Resource Allocation Use of grant funding Partnerships and collaborations Funding gaps and sustainability strategy Additional support needs beyond funding Key Dates and Timeline Application Opens: May 5, 2026 (9:00 AM EST) Information Session: May 7, 2026 (7:00 PM EST) Application Deadline: May 14, 2026 (8:00 PM EST) Funding Decisions Announced: June 24, 2026 Information Session A live workshop will be held to guide applicants through the process and answer questions. Details: Date: May 7, 2026 Time: 7:00 PM Eastern Time (US Canada) How to Apply Click here for more, to register and to apply Important notes: Account activation may take up to 2 days Applicants are encouraged to apply at least 3 days before the deadline Late submissions may not be considered Why This Grant Matters This funding opportunity plays a critical role in strengthening grassroots movements that protect and empower Black girls and women in the Southern United States. It provides essential unrestricted support that allows organizations to: Sustain vital advocacy work Respond to community needs in real time Expand justice-focused initiatives Strengthen leadership in marginalized communities For more global grant opportunities Click here The post Black Girls Defense Fund – Resistance and Resilience Grant 2026 (Up to $20,000 Funding Opportunity for Southern US Organizations) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: YLabs Youth Researcher Opportunity in Nigeria 2026 – Remote Field-Based Research Role for Early-Career Professionals
summary: YLabs is inviting applications from Nigeria-based early-career professionals for an exciting Youth Researcher opportunity focused on participatory and community-based research. This short-term contractor role offers candidates the chance to contribute to meaningful youth-centered projects while gaining hands-on experience in ethnographic research, design research, and field engagement. The organization is looking for individuals who are deeply connected to their communities and capable of gathering rich, context-driven insights through direct interaction with young people and local stakeholders. This role is ideal for emerging professionals interested in: Community engagement Participatory research Youth development Social impact Human-centered design Public health and development research Successful applicants will support field research activities across Nigeria while collaborating remotely with a global multidisciplinary team. About YLabs YLabs is a globally recognized design and research organization dedicated to amplifying youth power, agency, and opportunity through innovative technologies and inclusive research methods. Founded in 2014 at the Harvard Innovation Lab, YLabs partners directly with young people to co-create digital products and services that address pressing social challenges affecting youth globally. The organization works across more than 20 countries and focuses on: Youth health and wellness Economic opportunity Climate resilience Digital innovation Behavioral science Adolescent development YLabs combines expertise in: Human-centered design Epidemiology Digital product design Community engagement Research and behavioral science The organization is headquartered in Rwanda and the United States, with a globally distributed workforce. About the Youth Researcher Role The Youth Researcher role is designed for individuals with foundational experience in research and field engagement who can work directly within communities to uncover lived experiences, patterns, challenges, and opportunities. Researchers will support participatory research activities by: Conducting interviews Leading group discussions Carrying out observations Documenting field insights Participating in synthesis sessions The role requires a strong understanding of local contexts and the ability to respectfully engage individuals from diverse backgrounds. Contract Duration and Work Structure Employment Type Independent Contractor Work Location Nigeria (Remote with field-based activities) Contract Timeline Expected to run from May to Mid-July 2026 Work Style Primarily field-based Some remote collaboration required Key Responsibilities Selected Youth Researchers will support a range of participatory research activities. Core Duties Include: 1. Field Research Coordination Plan and coordinate field visits Manage schedules and local logistics Navigate community engagement processes 2. Conducting Research Activities Carry out one-on-one interviews Facilitate group discussions Conduct direct field observations 3. Community Engagement Build trust with youth and community stakeholders Create safe and respectful environments for discussion Encourage honest and open participation 4. Documentation and Insight Gathering Capture detailed field notes Record reflections and observations using structured tools Identify behavioral and environmental patterns 5. Data Reflection and Synthesis Participate in debrief sessions Surface emerging themes and recurring issues Help identify opportunities and tensions within communities 6. Ethical and Safe Research Practice Follow safeguarding and child protection guidelines Handle sensitive information responsibly Adhere to ethical research standards Ideal Candidate Profile YLabs is looking for candidates who are: Community-centered Curious and empathetic Strong listeners Respectful and culturally aware Reflective and open to learning Applicants should be able to connect authentically with people while maintaining professionalism and ethical standards. Required Qualifications and Experience Minimum Requirements Candidates should have: At least 2 years of experience in: Research Monitoring and evaluation Community engagement Design research Related development or social impact work This experience may come from: Education Professional work Training Community or lived experience Required Skills Applicants must demonstrate: Strong communication skills in English and relevant local languages Ability to conduct interviews and document findings clearly Strong observation and note-taking skills Comfort interacting with diverse community groups Ability to adapt to dynamic field environments Familiarity with digital tools and online collaboration platforms Confidential handling of information Understanding of ethical research and safeguarding principles Additional Advantage Experience working with: Major funders Philanthropic organizations Development organizations across Africa will be considered an added advantage. Compensation and Benefits Pay Rate Up to $11 per hour Compensation is based on experience Payment Method Monthly bank transfer payments YLabs follows Project Fair compensation principles, ensuring equitable pay standards based on work value and regional benchmarks. Eligibility Criteria This opportunity is specifically open to: Candidates based in Nigeria Individuals with strong understanding of local cultures and languages Applicants comfortable conducting in-person field engagement YLabs strongly encourages applications from: Women Persons living with disabilities Application Requirements Interested applicants must submit: Required Documents Resume/CV Short video introduction (1–2 minutes) What to Include in the Video The video should: Introduce your background Explain your connection to your community Discuss a community issue you care about Describe how you would investigate and understand the issue Explain: Who you would speak with How you would engage people What methods you would use to analyze information How to Submit the Video Applicants must: Upload the video to: Google Drive OR YouTube Ensure access settings are: “Anyone with the link can view” OR “Public” Paste the video link into the application form Important: Ensure the link works before submission Videos with restricted access cannot be reviewed YLabs emphasizes that production quality is not important. Applicants are encouraged to focus on: Clarity Organization Authenticity Application Deadline The deadline to apply is: 15 May 2026 Late or incomplete applications may not be considered. Click here for more and to apply Selection Process YLabs will: Review all submitted applications Contact shortlisted candidates during the hiring process Conduct background checks and criminal record clearances before final hiring decisions All applicant information will remain confidential. Equal Opportunity Statement YLabs is committed to promoting equity, inclusion, and belonging in the workplace. The organization welcomes applicants from all: Cultural backgrounds Educational backgrounds Socio-economic groups Gender identities Physical abilities Age groups YLabs maintains a strict non-discrimination policy. Why This Opportunity Matters This opportunity provides emerging researchers in Nigeria with: Real-world field research experience Exposure to global development work Experience in participatory and youth-centered design Collaboration with international experts Skills in ethnography and community engagement It is especially valuable for individuals seeking careers in: Development research Human-centered design Social innovation Public health Youth programming International development Click here for more global job opportunities The post YLabs Youth Researcher Opportunity in Nigeria 2026 – Remote Field-Based Research Role for Early-Career Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
